rules: RULE-01: input has level_id (look up LEVELS) or an inline level (array of grid-row strings). The grid uses '#' wall, ' ' floor, '.' target, '@' player, '$' box, '*' box-on-target, '+' player-on-target. Any other char is a generic tile stored in state.tiles (passable like floor unless a rule says otherwise). Apply input.moves in order, up to MAX_MOVES.
RULE-02: A move U/D/L/R steps the player one cell (DIRS). Moving into floor/target advances the player; log '<dir>:move'.
RULE-03: Moving into a wall '#' is blocked: nothing changes; log '<dir>:block'.
RULE-04: Moving into a box: if the cell beyond (same dir) is free (not wall, not another box) the box and player both advance; log '<dir>:push'. Otherwise blocked; log '<dir>:block'.
RULE-05: WIN when every box occupies a target cell. On win set isWon=true and isGameOver=true and stop processing remaining moves. on_target is the count of boxes currently on targets.

RULE-06 (THIS CARD): KEY and DOOR tiles. The level data (including levels with 'K'/'D' tiles) is ALREADY provided in the frozen levels.js — this card edits ONLY move_logic.js to add the behavior. A door tile 'D' is treated as a wall (blocks the player AND blocks a box being pushed into it) WHILE the player has no key. Stepping the player onto a key tile 'K' grants the key (state.hasKey=true), removes that 'K' from state.tiles, and appends '+key' to that move's log (e.g. 'R:move+key'); on a push that lands the player on a 'K', the suffix is '+key' too (e.g. 'R:push+key'). Once the key is held, 'D' tiles behave like floor. A box is never pushed onto a 'K' (treat it as blocked). Any play on a level with no K/D tiles produces byte-for-byte identical output to before this card.

files.description: Move resolution (rule core). THIS card adds the door-blocking check (closed 'D') to both the player step and the box-push destination, plus key pickup on entering a 'K' cell. Keep resolveMove(state, dir) signature and the existing wall/push/move behavior intact. levels.js is FROZEN (level data already present).
